event: recurrent midepigastric pain since his diagnosis with tropical pancreatitis
presented to a medical center in India with similar pain
CT scan of the abdomen revealed chronic atrophic calcific pancreatitis
return for removal of ductal stones planned
presented with a steady midepigastric pain of 6 hours duration
hospitalized for pain control with narcotics
fatty atrophy of both the pancreatic head and uncinate process
pancreatic duct was filled with large calcifications
possible obstruction at the level of the neck
marked upstream ductal dilatation of 1.5 cm
admitted for management of pain secondary to chronic pancreatitis
transferred to the medical intensive care unit
activated recombinant human protein C administered
ultrasound of the abdomen was done
no obvious obstructing calculi in the common bile duct
frank pus expelling spontaneously through the major papilla of Vater
evacuation of more than 5 ml of yellow pus

acute suppuration of the pancreatic duct (ASPD) assumed

inflammatory changes within the fat surrounding the body and tail of the pancreas consistent with edema
dilatation of the pancreatic duct had diminished

follow-up examination at 1 month after discharge
